criteria.label: Promote ad-hoc notebook/script code into a focused module with `__all__` and a clear public API.
criteria.example: Take a 60-line chunking cell, move it to `ragapp/chunker.py` exporting `chunk_text` and `Chunker`, and have the notebook, pipeline, and tests all import from there.

criteria.label: Keep import time near zero by deferring heavy work.
criteria.example: Replace a top-level `tiktoken.get_encoding(...)` with a `functools.cache`d `_tokenizer()` helper; verify `python -X importtime -c 'import ragapp.chunker'` drops from ~2s to ~10ms.

criteria.label: Design module APIs with dependency injection.
criteria.example: Refactor `summarize(text)` (which imports `openai.OpenAI()`) into `summarize(text, client)`; tests pass a fake client; production wires the real one.

criteria.label: Avoid circular imports by extracting shared concepts.
criteria.example: When `models.py` ↔ `db.py` cycle appears, create `schema.py` for the shared types; both depend on it, neither on each other.

criteria.label: Make a module dual-use (library + CLI) without surprising importers.
criteria.example: Wrap the CLI in `_cli(argv=None)`, guard with `if __name__ == '__main__': sys.exit(_cli())`; verify `python -m ragapp.chunker doc.txt` works and `from ragapp.chunker import chunk_text` doesn't run argparse.

criteria.label: Decide when *not* to split a module.
criteria.example: Resist splitting a coherent 300-line validator until a second caller appears with materially different needs; instead, improve names and section headers within the file.
